consents.title: LSCS CONSENT
consents.content:
मी_______________________________________________________________________वय________________वर्षे असून, माझी गरोदरपणाची__________________ खेप असून, आता___________________आठवडच्चाचा गर्भ राहिलेला आहे.आत्ता (Indication)___________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
असल्याने योग्य त्या भूलेखाली पोट चिरुन बाळ काढण्याची शस्त्रक्रिया करण्याचा सल्ला डॉक्टरांनी दिला आहे. शस्त्रक्रियेदरम्यान होणा-या गुंतागुंती, जसे की, जास्त रक्तस्त्राव होणे, मुत्राशय, मुत्रनलिका व आतड्यांना ईजा होण्याची शक्यता असल्याची पूर्वकल्पना डॉक्टरांनी दिली आहे. शस्त्रक्रियेदरम्यान वा  नंतर जास्त रक्तस्त्राव होऊन जीवास  धोका होऊ शकतो व रक्त चढवण्याची गरज लागू शकते. योग्य औषधोपचार व शस्त्रक्रिया करून रक्तस्त्राव न धांबल्यास एखादे वेळेस गर्भपिशवी काढावी लागू शकते. तसेच अतिदक्षता विभागात ठेवावे लागू शकते. टाक्यांमध्ये जंतुसंसर्ग होऊन टाके उघडू शकतात, तसेच भविष्यात होणारे बाळंतपणासाठी पुन्हा पोट चिरुन बाळ काढण्याची शस्त्रक्रिया करण्याची गरज लागू शकते. तसेच भूलेतून बाहेर येण्यास नेहमीपेक्षा जास्त वेळ लागू शकतो. शस्त्रक्रिया व भूलेच्या या सर्व धोक्यांची पूर्वकल्पना डॉक्टर व सिस्टरांनी मला समजेल अशा भाषेत दिली आहे. तसेच अॅनिमिया / गरोदरपणामुळे होणारा उच्च रक्तदाब / गर्भधारणेचा मधुमेह/  प्रीमॅच्युरिटी / अपुरे एनबीएम / ब्रॉन्कोस्पाझम / मागील एलएससीएस / प्लेसेंटा प्रेव्हिया / ट्रान्सव्हर्स प्रेझेंटेशन  यासारखे इतर उच्च जोखमीचे घटक उपस्थित आहेत आणि यामुळे रुग्णाची रुग्णता वाढू शकते तरी मी डॉक्टरांनी पोट चिरुन बाळ काढण्याची शस्त्रक्रिया करण्याची विनंती करीत आहे. वरील सर्व गोष्टी मला समजल्या आहेत.

operativeNotes.closure: Uterine incision was closed in two layers with 1-0 Vicryl. Rectus sheath closed with 1-0 Vicryl continuous sutures. Subcutaneous tissue closed with 3-0 Vicryl. Skin was closed with subcuticular 4-0 Monocryl.
operativeNotes.hemostasis: Hemostasis was achieved throughout the procedure using electrocautery and pressure. Total estimated blood loss was 500 mL. All swabs and instruments were accounted for.
operativeNotes.preOpDiagnosis: Pregnancy at 39 weeks gestation, previous cesarean section, patient Shobhana Bhamre.
operativeNotes.procedureDetails: Under spinal anesthesia, the patient was prepared and draped. Dr. Mohit Agrawal performed a Pfannenstiel incision. The abdomen was opened in layers. The bladder was reflected inferiorly. A transverse lower segment uterine incision was made. A live infant was delivered, followed by the delivery of the placenta via controlled cord traction. Uterine cavity was cleaned. No intraoperative complications were noted.
operativeNotes.postOpOrders: Monitor vitals every 15 minutes for 2 hours, then every 4 hours. Keep patient NPO for 6 hours. IV fluids at 125ml/hr. Administer IV analgesics and antibiotics as charted. Monitor uterine fundus and lochia. Encourage early ambulation after 12 hours.

consents.content:
शस्त्रक्रियेसाठी माहितीपूर्ण संमती पत्र (Informed Consent Form)

मी, {{PATIENT}}, याद्वारे डॉ. {{DOCTOR}} आणि त्यांच्या सहाय्यक टीमला माझ्यावर 'व्हजायनल हिस्टेरेक्टॉमी' (Vaginal Hysterectomy - योनीमार्गाद्वारे गर्भाशय काढणे) ही शस्त्रक्रिया करण्याची परवानगी देत आहे.

१. शस्त्रक्रियेचे स्वरूप:
मला असे सांगण्यात आले आहे की, या शस्त्रक्रियेमध्ये योनीमार्गातून गर्भाशय शस्त्रक्रिया करून काढून टाकले जाईल. या प्रक्रियेत पोटावर कोणताही मोठा छेद घेतला जात नाही.

२. शस्त्रक्रियेचे फायदे:
माझ्या सध्याच्या आरोग्यविषयक समस्यांचे (उदा. गर्भाशय खाली सरकणे, अतिरक्तस्त्राव किंवा गर्भाशयातील गाठी) निराकरण करणे हा या शस्त्रक्रियेचा मुख्य उद्देश आहे.

३. संभाव्य धोके आणि गुंतागुंत:
कोणत्याही शस्त्रक्रियेप्रमाणे यामध्येही काही धोके असू शकतात, जसे की:
- अति रक्तस्त्राव (Bleeding).
- संसर्ग (Infection).
- मूत्राशय (Bladder) किंवा आतड्यांना (Intestines) इजा होणे.
- भूल देण्यामुळे उद्भवणारे धोके (Anaesthesia complications).
- शस्त्रक्रियेदरम्यान तांत्रिक अडचण आल्यास, योनीमार्गाऐवजी पोट उघडून (Abdominal Hysterectomy) शस्त्रक्रिया करण्याची गरज पडू शकते.

४. पर्यायी उपचार:
मला उपलब्ध असलेल्या पर्यायी उपचारांची (उदा. औषधोपचार किंवा दुर्बिणीद्वारे शस्त्रक्रिया) माहिती देण्यात आली आहे, परंतु मी या शस्त्रक्रियेचा पर्याय निवडला आहे.

५. रुग्णाची घोषणा:
- डॉ. {{DOCTOR}} यांनी मला या शस्त्रक्रियेची गरज, फायदे, धोके आणि परिणामांची माहिती मराठी भाषेत सविस्तरपणे दिली आहे.
- मला माझ्या शंका विचारण्याची पूर्ण संधी मिळाली असून त्यांचे समाधानकारक निरसन झाले आहे.
- मी पूर्ण शुद्धीत आणि कोणत्याही दबावाशिवाय या शस्त्रक्रियेसाठी माझी संमती देत आहे.
- शस्त्रक्रियेदरम्यान किंवा त्यानंतर आपत्कालीन परिस्थिती उद्भवल्यास, डॉक्टरांनी योग्य तो निर्णय घेण्यास माझी संमती आहे.

admissionNote.generalExamination: Patient is conscious, cooperative, and oriented to time, place, and person. No evidence of pallor, icterus, or edema was found. General physical status appears stable despite elevated blood pressure recordings.
admissionNote.menstrualHistory: Patient reports previously regular menstrual cycles with no significant abnormalities prior to the current pregnancy.
admissionNote.planOfCare: Admission for labor monitoring. Strict monitoring of maternal blood pressure and fetal heart sounds. Prepare for Full Term Normal Delivery (FTND) while managing hypertensive status.
admissionNote.chiefComplaints: Pain in the abdomen at 9 months of gestation.
admissionNote.systemicExamination: Cardiovascular system: S1 S2 heard, no murmurs. Respiratory system: Normal vesicular breath sounds. Central Nervous System: No focal neurological deficits.
admissionNote.provisionalDiagnosis: Full Term Normal Delivery (FTND) in latent phase of labor with Pregnancy Induced Hypertension (PIH).
admissionNote.pastHistory: No significant past medical or surgical history reported. No known allergies.
admissionNote.historyOfPresentIllness: A 22-year-old female patient presented with a history of abdominal pain at 9 months of pregnancy. The pain is described as intermittent and progressively increasing in intensity, characteristic of labor.
admissionNote.localExamination: Obstetric examination shows a gravid uterus. Per-vaginal examination reveals the cervix is 1 cm dilated.
admissionNote.obstetricHistory: Patient is currently at 9 months of gestation (full term). This is a suspected case for Full Term Normal Delivery (FTND).

operativeNotes.anesthesiaNotes: normal
operativeNotes.postOpOrders: Monitor vital signs every 15 minutes for the first 2 hours, then every 4 hours. Maintain IV fluids at 125 mL/hr. Administer analgesics as per hospital protocol. Monitor urine output, uterine fundus height, and vaginal bleeding. Encourage early ambulation and initiate breastfeeding as tolerated.
operativeNotes.procedureDetails: The patient was brought to the operating room and placed in a supine position with a left lateral tilt. Spinal anesthesia was successfully administered by the anesthesiology team. After sterile preparation and draping of the abdomen, a Pfannenstiel skin incision was made. The incision was carried down through the subcutaneous tissue to the rectus fascia, which was divided. The rectus muscles were separated in the midline, and the peritoneum was entered. The lower uterine segment was identified, and a transverse incision was made. A healthy infant was delivered in a cephalic presentation and handed to the awaiting pediatric team. The placenta was delivered manually and was noted to be complete. The uterine cavity was explored and cleared. The uterine incision was closed in two layers with 1-0 Vicryl sutures. The visceral and parietal peritoneum were closed routinely.
operativeNotes.preOpDiagnosis: Term pregnancy, normal presentation, planned for elective Lower Segment Cesarean Section.
operativeNotes.hemostasis: Hemostasis was achieved using electrocautery and targeted sutures. Total estimated blood loss was approximately 500 mL. All counts for sponges and instruments were correct at the end of the procedure.
operativeNotes.closure: The fascia was closed with 1-0 Prolene sutures in a continuous fashion. The subcutaneous layer was irrigated, and the skin was closed using 3-0 Monocryl in a subcuticular technique. Sterile dressing was applied.

operativeNotes.closure: The uterine incision was closed in two layers using 1-0 Vicryl sutures. The visceral peritoneum was closed. The rectus sheath was closed with 1-0 Prolene sutures. Subcutaneous tissue was approximated with 2-0 Vicryl, and the skin was closed using subcuticular sutures.
operativeNotes.postOpOrders: Monitor vital signs every 15 minutes for the first 2 hours. Keep the patient NPO for 6 hours post-surgery. Administer IV fluids, analgesics, and prophylactic antibiotics. Monitor vaginal bleeding and uterine tone. Encourage early mobilization and breastfeeding.
operativeNotes.preOpDiagnosis: Term pregnancy at 39 weeks gestation, elective repeat cesarean section or maternal request.
operativeNotes.procedureDetails: Under spinal anesthesia, the patient was placed in a supine position with a left lateral tilt. Following sterile preparation and draping, a Pfannenstiel incision was made. The abdomen was entered in layers. A lower segment transverse uterine incision was performed. A healthy live infant was delivered and handed to the pediatrician. The placenta was removed completely via controlled cord traction. The uterine cavity was explored and found to be empty.

admissionNote.chiefComplaints: The patient, Nikita Agrawal, presents with complaints of abdominal pain.
admissionNote.planOfCare: Admit the patient for observation and monitoring. Monitor maternal vitals and fetal heart rate regularly. Conduct baseline investigations including a complete blood count and an obstetric ultrasound for fetal well-being. Assess for progression of labor.
